title: What did Shinji choose
wikitext:
[]: What did Shinji choose? Post by Gtamor » Sat Sep 08, 2012 3:56 pm I am having a hard time understanding what Shinji choose? Did he give every individual the option to either stick with instrumentality or return to earth? Why no one choose to return to earth (except for Asuka)? Thanks

[]: Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Sep 08, 2012 4:01 pm I think Yui said that anyone who has a strong enough sense of individuality will be able to emerge from the Sea of LCL. I'm not exactly sure why Shinji and Asuka were the only ones in that area who were able to return. I'll leave that for someone else to answer.

[]: Post by Justacrazyguy » Sat Sep 08, 2012 4:02 pm riffraff11235 wrote: I think Yui said that anyone who has a strong enough sense of individuality will be able to emerge from the Sea of LCL. I'm not exactly sure why Shinji and Asuka were the only ones in that area who were able to return. I'll leave that for someone else to answer.

[]: Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Sep 08, 2012 4:08 pm Justacrazyguy wrote: Asuka and Shinji were the first ones to come back. That´s it.

[]: Post by Justacrazyguy » Sat Sep 08, 2012 4:13 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Is that what it was? I remember reading somewhere that quite a bit of time had passed between the end of Instrumentality and the scene where we see Shinji and Asuka. At least a couple of weeks or so.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Sep 08, 2012 4:15 pm The answer depends on whether we are talking TV end or movie end (see the threads on concurrency). Movie end, Shinji's decision was to halt the process of Instrumentality. Having re-established the separation of individuals, they make their own decisions once again.

[]: Post by Der Kommissar » Sat Sep 08, 2012 4:56 pm It's not particularly relevant for any one else to be present in the last scene. Shinji and Asuka are stand-ins for greater humanity. One presumes that eventually, other people will chose to return. Asuka and Shinji are both a couple of kids that had deep-seated reasons to despise themselves and their lives, and given Instrumentality, they chose life. This is explicitly possible by word of Yui.

[]: Post by Dream » Sat Sep 08, 2012 5:54 pm In both endings it feels pretty clear that Shinji ends instrumentality and decides for everyone to come back as humans, a decision shared by the series and Anno himself. Yui said: "Anyone who can imagine themselves in their heart can come back to life [if memory doesn't fail me]" Whatever that can mean. And the final scene of EoE is extremely enigmatic and "surrealist" (at least how i understand surrealism) so i feel that applying a too logical or literal interpretation might be a misstep. I like the idea of Shinji and Asuka as stand-ins for greater humanity, along with the general content of Kommisar's post.

[]: Post by StratoSakuya...AF » Sat Sep 08, 2012 8:43 pm Shinji going nuts is purely speculation. One thing I know is that there wasn't any insanity involved on his behalf. He was strangling Asuka because he was blaming her for the current state of the world, he however realized he also was at fault and that it was beyond his control, and let go. People think time passed because he put gravemarkes, but they can be lifted in anything from hours to minutes, and the ones he put were for those who actually died. Not for the instrumentalized people, but for those who were killed on-screen.

[]: Post by Na7e » Sat Sep 08, 2012 9:01 pm StratoSakuya...AF wrote: Shinji going nuts is purely speculation.

[]: Post by StratoSakuya...AF » Sat Sep 08, 2012 9:15 pm Na7e wrote: SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by Der Kommissar » Sat Sep 08, 2012 9:31 pm People think time passed for a combination of factors. One, yes, he's put up graves. Two, one of those graves - Misato's - is actually marked with age: rust from the nail fastening her pendant to the marker has marred the pendant itself. This is the most obvious physical evidence of some amount of time passing. Three, Shinji's apparent demeanor has wildly changed from his stated acceptance and relatively calm facial expression to one that is wide-eyed and very obviously disturbed. It makes no sense to say that his expression is because he saw Rei if you continue to insist that little time has passed. He is, at this point, not at all perturbed seeing her in various bizarre forms and ways in a state of total undress. Hell, last we saw her she was straddling him and partially submerged in his body. Now, it might make sense for him to be reacting in shock to her if he hasn't seen anyone (let alone a Rei apparition) in weeks or even longer. As to why Shinji strangles Asuka? Well. Everyone has their own views. And there's a thread for that. I seriously doubt we'll ever get a fully 'canon' answer. I find your own answer less than optimal because Shinji has accepted responsibility for Instrumentality already, through the course of the event itself. He initially blamed Asuka and Rei/Lilith scolded him for doing so.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 08, 2012 9:37 pm Gtamor, check out these threads (and use the search and the thread list features to find ones of interest I might not have listed for you): http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/12649/Exactly-Which-Characters-Return-from-the-Sea-of-LCL/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/2512/The-People-Who-Dont-Return-From-3I/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/12703/After-3I-why-does-Asuka-regain-life-before-the-others/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/90/In-EoE-the-people-could-come-back/ Here's the thread addressing the "Shinji left alone for too long and goes crazy" thing: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/2270/Passage-of-Time-EoE-Final-Scene/ This looks like it's bound to become an enigmas-of-EoE potpourri thread, so I'm going to lock it, and folks can bump the older dedicated threads if they want to pursue a particular topic further.
summary:
[]: What did Shinji choose? Post by Gtamor » Sat Sep 08, 2012 3:56 pm I am having a hard time understanding what Shinji choose? Did he give every individual the option to either stick with instrumentality or return to earth? Why no one choose to return to earth (except for Asuka)? Thanks

[]: Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Sep 08, 2012 4:01 pm I think Yui said that anyone who has a strong enough sense of individuality will be able to emerge from the Sea of LCL. I'm not exact
